Sports Scene

Copied

In Sports Scene's exclusive interview series Talk Sports, CGTN's Zhu Mandan talks to the second fastest man ever, Jamaican sprinter Yohan Blake, who owns two Olympic gold medals in the relay and two individual silver medals.

Blake won three of his medals in London 2012, where he contributed to Jamaica's championship in the 4x100m relay, while winning individual silvers in the 100m and 200m events. Four years later at Rio 2016, he contributed again to defending Jamaica's victory in the 4x100m relay.

However, the 34-year-old recently fell short of a fourth Olympic appearance, after he placed fifth in the preliminary round of the 100m event in Jamaica's domestic trials, clocking 10.21 seconds which wasn't enough to send him trough.
